headline: GOLD SURGES to $4,761/oz All-Time High — Fiscal Anxiety, Dollar Weakness, and Central Bank Buying Fuel Historic April Rally
snippet: Gold reached $4,761/oz on April 14 — up $1,517 year-over-year — as fiscal and monetary anxiety, a weaker US dollar, and record central bank purchasing (WGC projects 900 tonnes in 2026) propel the metal to successive all-time highs.

headline: G Mining ACQUIRES G2 Goldfields in C$3B All-Stock Deal — Combined Guyana Hub Targets 500,000+ oz/yr Gold
snippet: G Mining Ventures is acquiring G2 Goldfields in an all-stock deal valuing G2 at ~C$3B, merging the adjacent Oko West and Oko-Ghanie deposits in Guyana into a single district-scale complex targeting 500,000+ oz/yr with over $1B in projected synergies.

headline: China Northern Rare Earth SLAMS Q2 Prices Up 44% — Seventh Consecutive Record Hike More Than Doubles Year-Ago Levels
snippet: China Northern Rare Earth set Q2 2026 concentrate prices at 38,804 yuan/tonne — up 44.6% on Q1 and more than double a year ago, the seventh consecutive increase under the unified pricing mechanism. Announcement 18 export controls on terbium, dysprosium, and yttrium remain fully in effect.

headline: GOLD-TO-SILVER RATIO Peaks Above 105 Then Contracts — GoldSeek SWOT Flags Historically Bullish Reversal as China Imports 790+ Tonnes
snippet: China imported 790+ tonnes of silver in early 2026, largely driven by solar manufacturing demand. The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 before contracting sharply — a historically reliable signal that has preceded sharp silver outperformance. Silver hit $77/oz on April 8.

headline: REalloys Signs Montana Rare Earth MOU — Locking In Domestic Supply 9 Months Before 2027 US Defense Cutoff on Chinese Material
snippet: REalloys (ALOY) has agreed to secure up to 10% of production from US Critical Materials Corp.'s Sheep Creek project in Montana — confirmed dysprosium, terbium, yttrium, and NdPr — ahead of US defense rules mandating removal of Chinese rare earths from the supply chain by 2027.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 171m at 0.83 g/t Au — Phase VI Intercepts Expand Oculto East Beyond Current Open Pit Resource Limit
snippet: AbraSilver's (TSXV: ABRA) Phase VI drill program at Diablillos, Argentina, returned 171.0m at 0.83 g/t Au and 10.3 g/t Ag from 230m depth, including 9m at 3.46 g/t Au. Results demonstrate expansion potential materially beyond the current open-pit constrained Mineral Resource.

headline: Orla Mining CONFIRMS 2km High-Grade Extension at Musselwhite — Stacked Lynx and PQ Zones Intersect Up to 27.5 g/t Au
snippet: Orla Mining (NYSE: ORLA) confirmed that Musselwhite's Mine Trend extends at least 2km further down plunge with stacked Lynx and PQ zones grading up to 27.5 g/t and 145 g/t Au. Reserve replacement and resource growth have shifted from aspirational to 'well-supported by drilling now in hand.'

headline: K92 Mining Delivers 44,022 oz Au in Q1 2026 — Record Lateral Development at Kainantu Clears Way for Stage 3 Expansion
snippet: K92 Mining (TSX: KNT) produced 46,743 oz AuEq (44,022 oz Au, 1.70M lbs Cu, 38,845 oz Ag) at Kainantu, Papua New Guinea in Q1 2026, on budget. Record lateral development now exceeds Stage 3 expansion requirements; full-year guidance of 190,000–225,000 oz AuEq reiterated.

headline: URANIUM HOLDS ABOVE $100/lb — AI Data Center Power Boom Emerges as Major Structural Driver for Nuclear Demand
snippet: Uranium spot prices surged past $100/lb in January 2026 and have remained near that level as AI-driven electricity demand reshapes nuclear power investment cases. The US Section 232 designation of uranium as a national security asset has opened the door to price floors and domestic producer incentives.

headline: NexGen Greenlights ROOK I CONSTRUCTION — Canada's Largest Uranium Deposit to Break Ground Summer 2026
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX: NXE) will start construction on Rook I in Saskatchewan this summer after receiving federal approval in March 2026. At ~30 million lb U₃O₈/yr in peak production over an 11-year mine life, it would be North America's highest-output uranium mine — first new Canadian uranium mine approved since 2004.

headline: COPPER CLIMBS to $6.01/lb — Up 30.6% Year-on-Year Despite LME Warehouse Stockpiles at Eight-Year High
snippet: Copper reached $6.01/lb on April 14, up 30.6% year-over-year, even as LME stockpiles climbed to an eight-year high. Tariff uncertainty and structural long-term supply deficits continue to support prices despite near-term demand softness signalled by rising inventories.

headline: Core Lithium Awards A$50M Mining Contract — Finniss Restart Set for May as Chinese Lithium Prices Jump 78%
snippet: Core Lithium (ASX: CXO) awarded a A$50M open-pit mining contract to NRW for Finniss's Grants deposit, with ore extraction starting May 2026 and first spodumene concentrate targeted Q3. The A$290M Glencore-backed restart coincides with Chinese lithium carbonate prices rising 78% since mid-December on VAT rebate changes.

headline: Silver Institute: SIXTH STRAIGHT Deficit Forecast for 2026 — 67 Moz Shortfall as Physical Investment Surges 20% to Three-Year High
snippet: The Silver Institute projects a 67 million oz supply deficit in 2026 — the sixth consecutive annual shortfall — as physical investment climbs 20% to 227 Moz, a three-year high. Total silver supply will reach a decade high of 1.05 billion oz, still insufficient to meet surging industrial and investment demand.
